title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 9397 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.

For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility, keep Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

At RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab Go messaging team handles case 1634 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed. In Go, multiple goroutines consuming one queue still implement competing consumers, not broadcast.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Late Subscriber Semantics happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Late Subscriber Semantics condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
